description: Authoritative Rhineland glyph contract for the Rhena/Viriditas notation platform. Single source of truth for glyph names, advance widths, SMuFL cross-references, and documentation. Consumed by the hildegard-neumes font project (scripts/generate-rhena-glyphs.py) via a pinned copy. Rhena's render_ir::glyphs::rhineland module MUST agree with this file; a unit test in Rhena asserts the match. Proposed by hildegard-neumes ADR-0003 (contract ownership). Pending Rhena-side adoption. IMPORTANT (2026-04-14 width review): the corrected widths rh_virga=90, rh_liquescent_asc=160, rh_liquescent_desc=160 land here first. As of this update Rhena's crates/rhena-core/src/render_ir/glyphs/rhineland.rs still has the pre-review (buggy) values 65/140/140. The coordinated Rhena-side adoption PR brings the width changes AND the diplomatic-mode golden-snapshot updates together in one commit. This contract is the single source of truth; Rhena's in-tree rhineland.rs catches up via that PR.

coordinate_system.comment: Font authored Y-up; Rhena's SVG backend flips to Y-down at render time via a transform. See hildegard-neumes rhena_integration_plan.md § 3.3.

glyphs.doc: Rhineland punctum: calligraphic parallelogram mark; basic single note.
Rhineland virga: thin vertical pen stroke with angular foot.
Small tilted diamond for climacus descending notes.
Wavy ornamental ascending note; 2–3 rounded calligraphic humps.
Ornamental repercussion note; small punctum body with a wavy tail. Directional split deferred to v2.
Apostropha head; repeated by the renderer for bistropha and tristropha.
Rhineland pressus: vertical line attached to a short undulating line (Gardiner p. 94). No SMuFL equivalent.
Ascending liquescent terminal; small head + upward calligraphic tail. Lighter stroke per Gardiner p. 92.
Descending liquescent terminal; small head + downward calligraphic tail.
Small diminutive punctum for diminutive-liquescent positions.
Rhineland movable C clef: horizontal dash above a calligraphic C.
Rhineland F clef for low-range antiphons.
Short phrase break; thin bar spanning one staff space.
Medium phrase break; thin bar spanning the full 4-line staff.
Long phrase break; thin bar extending above and below the staff.
Final double bar; two equal-weight thin verticals.
Small tick breath mark above the staff.
Ascending stroke connector for pes/scandicus joins. Private, no SMuFL.
Descending diagonal connector for flexa/clivis. Private, no SMuFL.
